target.title: Add per-agent sandbox session visibility override feature
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: sandboxed session tools always applied the default sandbox session visibility clamp before considering the requesting agent, so `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` could be configured but was not consistently honored.
- Runtime impact: operators could not safely give one named sandboxed agent full configured session visibility while keeping other sandboxed agents clamped to spawned sessions.
- Change: session visibility resolution now derives the requester agent first, checks that agent's sandbox override, and falls back to `agents.defaults.sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` only when no per-agent override exists.
- Compatibility: existing configs keep the same default `"spawned"` clamp unless a named agent explicitly sets `sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility`.

## User-Visible Behavior

New optional per-agent config:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "spawned" }
    },
    "list": [
      { "id": "tony", "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "all" } }
    ]
  }
}
```

For sandboxed session tools, precedence is:

```text
agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility
agents.defaults.sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility
default spawned clamp
```

## Security Impact

- Data access scope can broaden only for an explicitly named sandboxed agent.
- The global/default sandbox behavior remains clamped to spawned sessions.
- Existing `tools.sessions.visibility` and `tools.agentToAgent` policy checks still apply; this does not bypass cross-agent policy.
- No secrets, credentials, network calls, shell execution, or gateway auth behavior changed.

## Files in This PR

- `CHANGELOG.md` - records the user-facing runtime/config behavior change.
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md` - documents per-agent sandbox session visibility.
- `docs/gateway/config-tools.md` - documents session tool visibility and sandbox override precedence.
- `docs/tools/multi-agent-sandbox-tools.md` - documents multi-agent sandbox session tool behavior.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts` - wires requester identity into session tool construction.
- `src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware sandbox visibility to `session_status`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts` - centralizes requester-aware sandbox visibility resolution.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts` - forwards requester context through shared session helper wiring.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_history`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_list`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_send`.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts` - covers requester-aware `session_status` behavior.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts` - covers tool-factory wiring for the override.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` - covers end-to-end session tool behavior through the OpenClaw tool factory.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts` - covers access-layer override/fallback resolution.

## Verification

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=360000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts --reporter=dot`
  - Result: 10 test files passed, 268 tests passed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: a sandboxed named agent with `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility="all"` can use the configured `tools.sessions.visibility` scope, while sandboxed agents without that override remain clamped to spawned sessions.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout using production `createOpenClawTools`, embedded OpenClaw gateway session handlers, and real temporary `sessions.json` stores for `main` and `tony`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module -e <inline proof script>` from the repo root. The script created an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, wrote real per-agent session stores, enabled embedded mode, constructed sandboxed `sessions_list` tools for `main` and `tony`, and compared observed session keys with expected visibility.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "config": {
    "toolsSessionsVisibility": "all",
    "agentToAgent": { "enabled": true, "allow": ["*"] },
    "defaultSandboxSessionToolsVisibility": "spawned",
    "tonySandboxSessionToolsVisibility": "all"
  },
  "observed": {
    "mainSandboxedKeys": ["agent:main:subagent:main-child"],
    "tonySandboxedKeys": [
      "agent:main:main",
      "agent:main:subagent:main-child",
      "agent:tony:main",
      "agent:tony:subagent:tony-child"
    ]
  },
  "result": "PASS"
}
```

Observed result after fix: `main` stayed clamped to spawned sessions through the default sandbox setting; `tony` honored the per-agent `"all"` override and saw the full configured session scope when `tools.agentToAgent` allowed cross-agent access.

What was not tested: remote Crabbox/Testbox behavior. Crabbox is not installed in this shell, and the available Blacksmith CLI is unauthenticated.

## Risk and Rollback

- Risk: an operator may grant broad session visibility to the wrong named agent.
- Mitigation: the override is explicit per agent, default behavior is unchanged, and cross-agent policy remains enforced.
- Rollback: remove `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` from the agent entry to return to the default sandbox clamp.

target.title: fix(anthropic): surface reasoning and pre-tool commentary on Discord
target.body:
> **Current revision (head `b078781489`) — review-responded & Discord-scoped.** Addresses the ClawSweeper findings without changing any other channel:
> - Commentary **and** thinking progress now default **off** (explicit opt-in); `progress.thinking` is contract-wired into the type, zod schema, **and** the generated channel-config metadata (validates at config-load).
> - Message-tool-only reasoning-privacy invariant **restored** (trailing reasoning suppressed uniformly); opaque tool-call id keying restored.
> - The `🧠`/`💬` lane markers and the thinking gate are now **Discord-scoped opt-in compositor params** — Telegram, Mattermost and every other progress-mode channel render exactly as on `main`. This PR touches no other channel.
> - Verified live on Discord (`anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`), plain (`_p_`) + verbose/reasoning (`_rv_`). See the [review-response comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96106#issuecomment-4797749572) for the per-finding walkthrough.

---

## What Problem This Solves

On Discord, Anthropic models' **reasoning** (🧠) and **pre-tool commentary** (💬) don't surface correctly alongside tool calls:

- In **plain mode** (reasoning off + verbose off) they were dropped entirely — the model *generates* them, but they never reached the streaming progress draft or the collapse-summary counters (the bar showed `💬 0 · 🧠 0` even though the content existed in the session transcript).
- They didn't **interleave** with tool calls in arrival order — a multi-step turn collapsed multiple notes/thoughts onto a single line (edit-in-place) instead of showing `🧠 → 💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️` as the turn actually unfolded.

## Why This Change Was Made

`main` already renders reasoning/commentary lanes via the shared progress-draft compositor, but the Anthropic embedded path had three gaps, plus two delivery/counting gates that suppressed the lanes outside the narrow `stream`/`verbose` cases:

1. Anthropic tool-turn narration isn't tagged as commentary, so it's never recognized/routed.
2. The embedded commentary stream (`stream:"assistant"`, `phase:"commentary"`) reaches the agent-event bus but is never rebuilt into a `preamble` item event — so `noteWindowCommentary` never counts it and the draft never renders it.
3. Window thinking was gated on `reasoningMode === "stream"`, so plain mode (reasoning *off*) dropped reasoning from the window draft and counters. Window thinking is *presentation* — it should stream for any mode except `"on"` (which uses durable 🧠 persistence instead).

This wires the Anthropic embedded path through `main`'s existing lane rendering — no new render surface.

## User Impact

Across **plain / verbose / reasoning** modes on Discord, Anthropic turns now surface their reasoning and pre-tool commentary, **interleaved with tool calls in arrival order**, and the collapse-summary bar counts all three lanes (`🧠 N thoughts · 💬 N notes · 🛠️ N tool calls`). No config changes; no new render surface.

## Evidence

Live, on a build carrying these commits (Discord, `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`), verified by reading the bot's actual channel messages:

**Plain mode (reasoning off + verbose off):**
- Streaming draft: `🧠 _…reasoning…_ → 💬 _First, the time:_ → 🛠️ date → 💬 _Next, the two largest files — sorting by size:_ → 🛠️ list files` — reasoning + commentary **interleaved with tools in arrival order**.
- Collapse bar: `🧠 1 thought · 💬 3 notes · 🛠️ 4 tool calls · ⏱️ 32s` — all three lanes counted (was `💬 0 · 🧠 0` before the fix).

**Verbose + reasoning mode:** commentary renders as durable messages interleaved with tools: `💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️`. No regression.

**After-fix evidence (plain mode, reasoning off + verbose off):**

Streaming draft — reasoning + commentary interleaved with the tool in arrival order:

![plain streaming interleave](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/f7aaac7bbff42c90878a626828ce2b004f90b50e/pr-proof/96106/96106-plain-streaming-interleave.png)

Collapse summary — all three lanes counted:

![plain collapse counts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/f7aaac7bbff42c90878a626828ce2b004f90b50e/pr-proof/96106/96106-plain-collapse-counts.png)


**Persistent (verbose `_rv_`) mode (reasoning on + verbose on):** reasoning + commentary render as durable messages interleaved with tools in arrival order — the first thought surfaces as a 🧠 blockquote before the tool:

![rv persistent interleave](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/22439bc71b08fd485de9ec0bac89cbb916275831/pr-proof/96106/96106-rv-persistent-interleave.png)

Validation: `tsgo` (core + test-types) pass; lane suites green (`message-handler.process.test.ts`, `embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`, `progress-draft-compositor.test.ts` including the `"interleaves reasoning bursts with tool calls in arrival order"` assertion); `build-all` exit 0.

## Linked context

- **Fixes #96079.**
- **Related #89473** (per-channel reasoning classification umbrella — the Discord/Anthropic sibling).

target.title: fix: keep yielded parent runs deferred until subagents settle
target.body:
## Summary

This change promotes yielded-parent semantics into a shared parent lifecycle classifier and routes the affected cron, subagent-registry, and Discord/source-message paths through that state instead of letting each consumer infer completion from terminal-shaped model-attempt events, missing final text, or abort-shaped control flow.

The shipped behavior is:

- yielded parent + active descendants is treated as waiting/deferred, not ordinary completion;
- yielded parent + no descendant is treated as diagnostic error;
- cron delivery waits for a real final outcome instead of counting the yielded attempt as `ok / not-delivered`;
- Discord/source-message progress remains parent-owned while the yielded parent is waiting;
- descendant settlement wakes/reconciles yielded parents through the existing continuation paths.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where parent-agent runs that spawned child sessions/subagents and then called `sessions_yield` could look finished before the parent task actually completed.

The most visible failures were:

- isolated cron `agentTurn` jobs with valid delivery targets could finish as `ok / not-delivered` because the yielded parent had no final payload yet;
- Discord source-message lifecycle could clear parent progress/done indicators while child work was still active or while the parent was waiting to reconcile final output;
- run history/status surfaces made yielded parent work look like delivery failure, ordinary success with no output, or completed source-message work instead of a paused/resumable parent lifecycle.

`session_yield` is supposed to end the current model turn, not the parent task. This PR makes that distinction explicit for the affected runtime consumers.

## User Impact / Intended Outcome

Operators and developers can now rely on `sessions_yield` after spawning child/subagent work without the parent run being misrepresented as complete at the cron or Discord lifecycle boundary.

Practically:

- autonomous development crons stop inflating misleading `ok / not-delivered` results for intentionally yielded runs;
- cron history exposes a distinct `deferred` state for yielded parent handoff;
- Discord progress no longer marks the parent source message done just because the parent yielded while children continue;
- yielded parent runs are resumed/reconciled when descendant work settles;
- debugging points to the parent/child lifecycle state instead of falsely implying delivery target resolution failed.

## Linked Context

- #95535 — `sessions_yield` isolated cron resumption/delivery gap.
- #92369 — subagent orchestration in isolated cron sessions needs reliable spawn/wait/aggregate semantics.
- #92116 — `sessions_yield` completion delivered as passive context can break orchestration loops.
- #86537 — subagent terminal reconciliation can expire suspended final delivery.
- #86538 — session write-lock timeouts can block subagent delivery lanes.
- #95833 — subagent abort-settle/session-lock failure can break session continuation.
- #92460 — isolated cron completion delivery gap around final controller return.
- #95604 — Discord subagent progress visibility.
- #94153 — durable queued subagent completion announcements.
- #95847 — requester-consumed descendant completion accounting.

## What Changes

### Shared parent lifecycle state

- Adds `src/agents/agent-lifecycle-parent-state.ts`.
- Classifies lifecycle events with explicit yielded/waiting vs terminal semantics.
- Exports the classifier through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/reply-runtime`.
- Routes subagent registry and Discord source-message handling through the classifier instead of local `yielded && paused` inference.

### Cron lifecycle and delivery

- Adds first-class cron run status `deferred`.
- Classifies yielded descendant handoff before delivery-mode branching, so text, structured payloads, `delivery.mode: none`, source-satisfied delivery, and normal direct delivery all share the same semantics.
- Prevents duplicate/interim parent announcements while descendants are active.
- Records yielded-without-descendant as explicit error diagnostic.
- Keeps deferred manual task runs active rather than failing them.
- Disables one-shot deferred cron jobs after handoff to avoid retry loops.

### Persistence, protocol, CLI, and UI

- Extends run-log JSON/SQLite encoding and status filtering for `deferred`.
- Extends Gateway protocol schemas and cron server filters.
- Extends CLI status/wait typing and docs.
- Extends hook-visible status typing.
- Adds Control UI status/filter labels and generated locale fallbacks.

### Parent/subagent reconciliation

- Tracks yielded/paused parents that still have pending descendants.
- Marks yielded parents with `wakeOnDescendantSettle` so they are resumed/reconciled when descendant work settles.
- Handles child success/failure settlement through existing subagent lifecycle recovery paths.
- Adds regression coverage for yielded parents being reconciled after child failure so diagnostics can wake the parent.

### Discord/source-message lifecycle

- Projects lifecycle events from agent execution into reply/channel handling.
- Uses the shared classifier to identify yielded/waiting parent state.
- Preserves in-progress/progress representation for yielded parents with active children.
- Avoids prematurely marking Discord status reactions done or clearing draft/progress state when the parent yielded and remains waiting.

### Concurrent user-message semantics

`docs/tools/subagents.md` documents the selected policy: if a human sends another message while a requester is yielded, the new message starts a normal turn for the same requester session. OpenClaw keeps the yielded run visible as waiting on descendant completion rather than converting it into a stale final reply; when descendant completion arrives, the completion event is handed back to the requester for synthesis.

## Implementation Notes

Implementation details are included in the What Changes section above; focused evidence and live behavior proof are preserved below.

## Tests / Verification

Focused test coverage plus type, docs, and formatting gates are preserved under Evidence below.

## Evidence

### Rebase/Squash/Proof

- Common upstream base: `13ecca5408cbc94e66105608bfe770d0fc660d76` (`origin/main`, `fix(telegram): back off session init spool retries`)
- Previous PR head: `c163d18fa610687e7dfb8585f1fe8f30b0c4185d`
- Current PR head: `73cff6aa1b0a3ab5786fdfc3a96c98f575e7fcc8`
- Rebased and squashed to one commit on the common base; pushed with `--force-with-lease`.

### ClawSweeper Requested Real Behavior Proof Coverage

ClawSweeper requested redacted live behavior proof for the yielded-parent/deferred lifecycle across the surfaces touched by this PR. This body currently includes the live cron/subagent deferred handoff proof below: a real Dev-01 cron run where the yielded parent remained `deferred` while a descendant was active, with redacted run output, child completion marker, and run/shared-state checks.

Remaining ClawSweeper-requested proof not present in this body yet:

- Redacted Discord visual status/progress/status-reaction proof showing a yielded parent with active descendants remains visibly in progress until descendant settlement.
- Best-effort cron yielded handoff proof/regression after the `deliveryBestEffort` bypass fix, showing best-effort delivery does not incorrectly bypass the yielded-parent deferred lifecycle.
- Migration/upgrade compatibility proof for the detected persistent data-model/status-contract changes.

Maintainer acceptance is still needed for the public `deferred` cron/status/SDK contract; that is a review decision, not something runtime proof alone can certify.

### Real / Live Behavior Proof

Runtime: Dev-01 installed stack commit `5c1fd341c89b1493fed1ea6014388394291450c3` via `/home/kklouzal/update.sh`; `openclaw --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (5c1fd34)` and `openclaw-gateway.service` was active from that stack.

Focused live cron/subagent handoff run:

```bash
openclaw cron add --json \
  --name pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z \
  --at +6h --keep-after-run --session isolated --no-deliver \
  --tools sessions_spawn,sessions_yield --timeout-seconds 600 \
  --message 'Live proof marker pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z ...'

openclaw cron run 514fd6e1-862e-43ca-87e3-da1197a7c938 \
  --wait --wait-timeout 12m --poll-interval 5s --timeout 600000
```

Redacted observed output from the live run:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "enqueued": true,
  "runId": "manual:514fd6e1-862e-43ca-87e3-da1197a7c938:1782253518340:1",
  "completed": true,
  "status": "deferred",
  "run": {
    "status": "deferred",
    "diagnostics": {
      "summary": "cron isolated agent yielded while descendant runs are still active",
      "entries": [
        {
          "source": "delivery",
          "severity": "warn",
          "message": "cron isolated agent yielded while descendant runs are still active"
        }
      ]
    },
    "deliveryStatus": "not-requested",
    "delivery": { "fallbackUsed": false, "delivered": false },
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:cron:<job-id>:run:<session-id>"
  }
}
cron_run_exit=1
```

The non-zero wait exit is expected for this proof: `openclaw cron run --wait` exits non-zero for persisted `deferred` status, while still returning the run record. Follow-up inspection showed the spawned child completed with marker `CHILD_MARKER pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z` and the shared state row recorded `ended_reason = subagent-complete`. The cron shared-state row for the same job persisted `last_run_status = deferred`, `last_delivery_status = not-requested`, and diagnostic summary `cron isolated agent yielded while descendant runs are still active`.

Proof sources:

- CLI run output captured in the maintainer live shell on Dev-01.
- Cron run log: `openclaw cron runs --id 514fd6e1-862e-43ca-87e3-da1197a7c938 --limit 5`.
- Shared state DB checks scoped to that job/run in `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
- Child/session redacted traces scoped to `agent:main:cron:<job-id>:run:<session-id>` and `agent:main:subagent:<child-session-id>`.

Remaining proof gap: this live proof covers the cron yielded-parent/subagent deferred lifecycle path. It does not provide Discord visual status-reaction proof.

### Focused test coverage

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/agent-lifecycle-parent-state.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts \
  ui/src/ui/views/cron.test.ts \
  src/cron/service/ops.test.ts \
  src/cron/service.persists-delivered-status.test.ts \
  src/cron/run-log.test.ts \
  packages/gateway-protocol/src/cron-validators.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
```

Passed.

Coverage includes:

- shared parent lifecycle classifier behavior;
- yielded cron parent + active descendants -> `deferred`;
- yielded cron parent + no descendant -> diagnostic `error`;
- structured/no-delivery/source-satisfied delivery paths classified before delivery;
- no duplicate parent interim delivery while descendants are active;
- deferred run-log persistence and filtering;
- deferred Gateway protocol validators;
- manual `cron.run` deferred task-ledger behavior;
- one-shot deferred cron disabling;
- Control UI deferred status/filter rendering;
- CLI wait/status behavior for deferred;
- yielded parent descendant-settle wake/reconciliation;
- yielded parent child-failure diagnostics;
- Discord yielded-parent progress preservation;
- plugin SDK subpath export coverage for the classifier.

### Type, docs, and formatting gates

```bash
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:test:src --noEmit
```

target.title: fix(media): resolve absolute-path channel media for native vision blocks
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram inbound images saved to disk are not passed to the model as native vision content blocks. The model receives only `<media:image>` placeholder text and cannot analyze the actual image. The same workflow works correctly through the webchat / Control UI.
- Why now: Any user sending images via Telegram DM to an OpenClaw bot with a vision-capable model cannot get the agent to see or describe the image. The image file is correctly downloaded to `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/` but is silently dropped during native vision block hydration.
- What changed:
  - `MediaAttachmentCache.resolveLocalPath()` now validates absolute filesystem paths against local roots via `resolveUsableLocalCandidate`, matching the existing relative-path validation added in 610691c796
  - `resolveCurrentTurnImages()` now returns successfully resolved images even when a subset fails, instead of discarding the entire batch
  - Added regression test for absolute-path media hydration
- What did NOT change: Relative-path handling is unchanged. Webchat inline-image path (early-return on `params.images`) is untouched. No new dependencies added.
- Outcome: Channel-delivered absolute-path images (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) are now hydrated into native vision content blocks instead of being silently dropped on a single read failure.
- Reviewer focus: `attachments.cache.ts` changes to `resolveLocalPath`, `current-turn-images.ts` changes to the all-or-nothing fallback logic, and the new test case.

## Linked context

Closes #93816 

Was this requested by a maintainer?
- No, but the issues have clear reproduction steps and root cause analysis.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `resolveLocalPath()` skipped absolute-path validation — it returned `path.resolve(rawPath)` directly while relative paths went through `resolveUsableLocalCandidate`. Channel-delivered inbound media (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) use absolute filesystem paths from `saveMediaBuffer`. When the path later failed `isInboundPathAllowed` in `ensureLocalStat`, the error was silently caught — channel attachments have `url` cleared, so `getBuffer` had no fallback and re-threw. Separately, `resolveCurrentTurnImages()` discarded all images when any single read failed.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test coverage for absolute-path media hydration. The existing test only covered relative paths. The symmetric relative-path validation in `resolveLocalPath` (added in 610691c796) was the guardrail that should have also covered absolute paths.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram inbound images saved to disk are not passed to the model as native vision content blocks; the model receives only `<media:image>` placeholder text and cannot analyze the actual image.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu 24.04 (x64), Node 22.22.0, OpenClaw main branch (9166165).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.test.ts
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs output:
  ✓ hydrates Telegram-style state-relative media into native prompt images
  ✓ hydrates channel-delivered absolute-path media into native prompt images
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  2 passed (2)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The new absolute-path test passes alongside the existing relative-path test. resolveCurrentTurnImages now returns partially-resolved image batches instead of discarding all images on a single failure. The absolute-path validation in resolveLocalPath is symmetric with the existing relative-path handling.
- What was not tested: Not run against a live Telegram bot with a real image. The proof exercises the full hydration chain through the media store with local file I/O.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Unit-test-based — does not verify a live end-to-end Telegram roundtrip.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/media-understanding/runner.attachments.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?
- Added 1 new test case:
  1. `hydrates channel-delivered absolute-path media into native prompt images` — writes a file under a temp state/media directory, sets `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and asserts absolute-path hydration into native `ImageContent` blocks

What failed before this fix, if known?
- Telegram inbound images appeared only as `<media:image>` placeholder text
- Any channel using `saveMediaBuffer` for inbound media could silently lose native vision blocks when a single attachment read failed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(vydra): bound control response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Vydra success-path untrusted body handling read submit and job-poll control JSON with unbounded `await response.json()`, so a hostile, misconfigured, or SSRF-reachable endpoint could stream a no-`Content-Length` body indefinitely and force OpenClaw to buffer it before parsing, causing OOM pressure or a hang on image, video, and speech generation paths.

The affected reads were:

- `extensions/vydra/image-generation-provider.ts` image submit JSON
- `extensions/vydra/speech-provider.ts` speech submit JSON
- `extensions/vydra/video-generation-provider.ts` video submit JSON
- `extensions/vydra/shared.ts` shared job poll JSON used by image/video async jobs

## Changes

- Replace those four success-path `response.json()` calls with the shared `readProviderJsonResponse` helper from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http`.
- Keep the fix plugin-scoped and reuse the existing 16 MiB provider JSON cap; no new abstraction.
- Preserve the existing `assertOkOrThrowHttpError` behavior, job id/status extraction, asset download path, and `release()` cleanup.
- Add focused Vydra regression tests for oversized image submit, speech submit, video submit, and shared poll JSON responses.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Vydra submit and job-poll control JSON from an untrusted external endpoint must not be buffered whole on the success path. Oversized no-`Content-Length` bodies must stop at the 16 MiB cap, cancel the stream, and throw a bounded `...exceeds 16777216 bytes` error. Small valid JSON must continue to parse normally.
- **Real environment tested**: A real `node:http` server (`createServer`) bound to `127.0.0.1`, streaming JSON in 64 KiB chunks with no `Content-Length` header and a would-stream size of about 64 MiB. The script drove the real exported `buildVydraImageGenerationProvider().generateImage()` over a real loopback socket through the real `postJsonRequest`, guarded fetch path, and `readProviderJsonResponse`. The shared poll helper was exercised by returning a queued submit payload and then streaming the oversized poll response from `/jobs/<id>`. Node v22.22.0.
- **Exact steps**: `node --import tsx scratchpad/vydra-bound-proof.mts` — start the streaming server → call real Vydra image submit and assert bounded throw + server-side socket abort near the cap → call real Vydra image job poll path and assert bounded throw + socket abort near the cap → run a negative control using old unbounded `fetch().arrayBuffer()` against the same streaming body → call the real provider against a small valid JSON response and asset response.
- **Observed result**: submit JSON threw `Vydra image generation: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes` and aborted after 20,119,564 bytes; shared poll JSON threw `Vydra job status: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes` and aborted after 18,022,412 bytes. The negative control buffered the full 67,108,866 bytes, proving the cap is load-bearing. The small happy path returned the generated image payload and metadata.
- **What was not tested**: I did not hit the live Vydra service; live endpoints would not reproduce a hostile oversized body. The real socket proof covers the transport and cancellation behavior. The TCP proof uses the image provider to exercise submit and shared poll because Vydra image requests expose the request-level SSRF policy needed for local loopback proof; speech/video submit bounding is covered by the focused in-repo Vitest regressions using the same shared reader and error text. The proof script is not committed.

## Evidence

Real `node:http` terminal proof (real loopback socket, real exported Vydra provider, real bounded reader):

```
[proof] real node:http server on http://127.0.0.1:39391/api/v1, cap=16777216 bytes, would-stream≈67108864 bytes per overflow route

PASS  image submit JSON: overflow throws bounded error :: threw=true msg="Vydra image generation: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
PASS  image submit JSON: stream cancelled near 16MiB :: aborted=true bytesSent=20119564 (<33554432)
PASS  shared poll JSON: overflow throws bounded error :: threw=true msg="Vydra job status: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
PASS  shared poll JSON: stream cancelled near 16MiB :: aborted=true bytesSent=18022412 (<33554432)
PASS  negative control: OLD unbounded read buffers PAST the 16MiB cap :: buffered=67108866 bytes (> 16777216)
PASS  negative control consumed far more than bounded reads :: unbounded buffered=67108866 vs image submit sent≈20119564
PASS  happy path: small submit JSON still parses and asset download still works :: jobId=job-happy bytes=8 status=completed

[proof] 7 PASS / 0 FAIL
[proof] ALL PASS
```

Focused Vitest after rebase:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/vydra/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/vydra/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/vydra/speech-provider.test.ts

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  16 passed (16)
[proof] focused Vydra Vitest exit=0
```

Type and format checks after rebase:

```
pnpm tsgo:extensions
[proof] post-rebase tsgo:extensions exit=0

pnpm tsgo:test:extensions
[proof] post-rebase tsgo:test:extensions exit=0

pnpm format:check -- extensions/vydra/image-generation-provider.ts extensions/vydra/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/vydra/shared.ts extensions/vydra/speech-provider.ts extensions/vydra/speech-provider.test.ts extensions/vydra/video-generation-provider.ts extensions/vydra/video-generation-provider.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check
# clean
```

Autoreview after rebase:

```
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
```

target.title: fix(speech): bound TTS response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

speech/TTS success-path 之前会对不可信响应体执行无界 `await response.text()` / `await response.json()`，音频生成 JSON/base64 或 Seed frame 随音频长度膨胀时可能导致 OOM/hang。

## Changes

- 将 Volcengine Seed Speech success response 从无界 `response.text()` 改为共享 `readResponseWithLimit`，16MiB cap，overflow 时 cancel stream。
- 将 Volcengine legacy TTS success response 从无界 `response.text()` 改为共享 `readResponseWithLimit`，16MiB cap，overflow 时 cancel stream。
- 将 Xiaomi TTS success response 从无界 `response.json()` 改为共享 `readProviderJsonResponse`，复用 provider JSON 16MiB cap。
- 增加 Volcengine Seed/legacy 与 Xiaomi TTS oversized success body 单测。

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
- Volcengine Seed Speech success body、Volcengine legacy TTS success body、Xiaomi TTS success JSON 都不再无界读取。
- overflow 时真实 fetch 响应流被 cancel，node:http loopback proxy 看到 socket abort。
- 小 JSON happy-path 仍正常解析。
- 负向 control 证明旧式无界 `.text()` 会完整 buffer 超过 cap 的 body。

Real environment tested:
- 本地 worktree `fix/bound-speech-tts`，基于最新 `upstream/main` rebase 后执行。
- 真实 `node:http` loopback proxy + production `fetchWithSsrFGuard` 路径；目标 URL 使用 `http://example.com`，代理把响应转成本机无 Content-Length 流式 body。

Observed result:

target.title: fix(openrouter): bound media response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Success-path untrusted OpenRouter image/video response bodies used unbounded reads, which could OOM or hang the process if a provider streamed a large JSON body without Content-Length.

## Changes
- Replace the OpenRouter image success-path `response.json()` read with shared `readProviderJsonResponse` bounded parsing.
- Replace the OpenRouter video submit/poll success-path JSON read with shared `readProviderJsonResponse`, while preserving the existing malformed-response error for invalid JSON.
- Let OpenRouter video use the existing provider request config for private-network allowance instead of forcing the resolved request config to `false`; the default remains denied through the shared config helper.
- Add focused OpenRouter image/video tests for the bounded helper path and oversized submit-body cancellation.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed
- OpenRouter image and video success-path JSON responses now stop at the shared 16MiB cap and abort the streaming TCP response when exceeded.

Real environment tested
- Local Node.js v22.22.0 process using `node:http` loopback TCP servers, no `Content-Length`, true `fetch`, and the real exported OpenRouter image/video provider builders.

Exact steps
- `node --import tsx scratchpad/openrouter-bound-proof.mjs`

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`

Observed result
- Bounded OpenRouter image/video success JSON throws `exceeds 16777216 bytes`, aborts the server socket, and sends fewer bytes than the full oversized body.
- Raw `response.json()` negative control buffers the full oversized body.
- Small OpenRouter image/video JSON responses parse normally.

What was not tested
- Live OpenRouter API calls.
- The optional OpenRouter generation-cost lookup was not changed in this PR.

## Evidence

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve abort after dispatch rejection
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Preserves the terminal `chat.abort` outcome when `chat.send` dispatch rejects after abort already completed.
- Preserves the real dispatch `error` outcome when abort arrives only during awaited error persistence, instead of letting a late abort mask/cache the error.
- Keeps current `main` dispatch-error lifecycle behavior intact: pre-agent dispatch errors still persist failed session state and emit `sessions.changed`.
- Adds regressions for both race orderings and prevents stale abort tombstones from leaking into later same-id retries.

Why does this matter now?

- `chat.send` caches terminal abort results for abort races. The post-dispatch rejection path still had two bad windows: abort-first could be overwritten by a late dispatch error, and error-first could expose `aborted` while the handler was awaiting fallback persistence.
- The conflict refresh also picked up the current `main` dispatch-error lifecycle path, so the merge has to preserve both the abort/error terminal-state contract and the session lifecycle contract.

What is the intended outcome?

- Same-run retry after an abort-first race observes the terminal timeout/aborted payload, not a synthetic dispatch error.
- Same-run retry after an error-first race observes the dispatch error while the error dedupe is live; a later retry can start normally after error dedupe cleanup.
- Late abort in the error-first path returns `aborted:false`, emits no aborted chat state, and does not recreate the abort tombstone.
- Pre-agent dispatch error continues to persist failed session lifecycle state and emit `sessions.changed` with `reason:"chat.dispatch-error"`.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Client-side terminal ACK handling.

target.title: fix(agents): use appropriate log levels in model-resolver fallback paths
target.body:
## Summary

Fix severity misclassification of log statements in `model-resolver.ts` fallback paths. This is a follow-up to the log-level cleanup in `tools-manager.ts` and `resource-loader.ts` (PR #89982), completing the same fix pattern across the remaining affected file in the same subsystem.

## Changes

Three log-level corrections in `src/agents/sessions/model-resolver.ts`:

1. **Line 587** — `console.error` → `console.warn`: The model restoration failure is recoverable (runtime continues with a fallback model), not a fatal error
2. **Line 597** — `console.log` → `console.info`: Routine fallback progress message
3. **Line 618** — `console.log` → `console.info`: Second fallback path progress message

## Severity Classification

The project maps `console.log` → `"info"` level, `console.warn` → `"warn"` level, `console.error` → `"error"` level (see `src/logging/console.ts:302-305`). The fixes align the severity with the actual impact:

| Location | Before | After | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| L587 | `console.error` | `console.warn` | Recoverable fallback — runtime continues with fallback model |
| L597 | `console.log` | `console.info` | Routine progress (user already saw the warning, no new action needed) |
| L618 | `console.log` | `console.info` | Routine progress (same) |

## Verification

TypeScript syntax verified. Full type checking via CI pipeline.

target.title: feat(fs): allow configured workspace aliases
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where operators using `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` could not safely keep shared content such as `memory/` outside the per-agent workspace behind an in-workspace symlink. In the layout captured by #90977, memory flushes and file-tool writes to `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` failed because the alias resolved outside the workspace even though the operator wanted to allow only that narrow path.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change adds an explicit `tools.fs.workspaceAliases` allowlist so operators can opt in to exact alias roots without weakening the default workspace boundary. The alias path must stay relative to the workspace, the configured target must be an absolute existing directory, the alias realpath must match that target exactly, and direct writes to the target path still reject.

## User Impact

Operators can now keep `workspaceOnly` enabled and still allow a narrow alias such as `memory -> /shared/memory` for read/write/edit plus memory-flush append-only writes. Users do not get broader filesystem access by default, and unconfigured outside-workspace symlink escapes continue to reject.

## Evidence

- Ran a live local runtime proof on the PR branch using the real `createOpenClawCodingTools` path with `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` plus `tools.fs.workspaceAliases: [{ path: "memory", target: outsideDir }]`.
- Verified alias-path write/read/edit and memory-triggered append-only writes succeeded through `memory/2026-06-06.md` while direct writes to the outside target path still rejected.
- Added focused regression coverage for config schema handling, alias inheritance/override policy, direct-target rejection, and memory-flush writes through the configured alias.
- Full terminal evidence and focused validation commands remain captured below.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90977

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #38622
Related #7722
Related #85876

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No maintainer explicitly requested this implementation. ClawSweeper kept #90977 open and flagged it for maintainer/product/security review, with a narrow exact-realpath alias called out as the likely fix shape.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw.write`/memory flush can append to `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` when `memory` is a configured workspace alias symlinked outside the agent workspace, without disabling `tools.fs.workspaceOnly`.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout of this PR at `7c865a65e0fc413e69f73bbefda1bbfbcd75748c`, using the actual `createOpenClawCodingTools` runtime path against real temporary filesystem directories and symlinks.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `./node_modules/.bin/tsx` inline proof script that imported `createOpenClawCodingTools`, configured `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` plus `tools.fs.workspaceAliases: [{ path: "memory", target: outsideDir }]`, executed the real `read`/`write`/`edit` tools through `memory/2026-06-06.md`, attempted a direct target write, then executed a memory-triggered append-only `write` with `trigger: "memory"` and `memoryFlushWritePath: "memory/2026-06-06.md"`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
PR #90986 live file-tool proof on local Linux checkout
head: 7c865a65e0fc413e69f73bbefda1bbfbcd75748c
workspace: <tmp-openclaw-ws-alias-proof>/workspace
alias path: memory -> <tmp-openclaw-ws-alias-proof>/memory-target
configured target: <tmp-openclaw-ws-alias-proof>/memory-target
alias realpath matches configured target: true
alias write result: Successfully wrote 12 bytes to memory/2026-06-06.md
alias read contains seed memory: true
direct target write rejected: yes: Path escapes sandbox root (<tmp-openclaw-ws-alias-proof>/workspace): <tmp-openclaw-ws-alias-proof>/memory-target/2026-06-06.md. Use a relative path under `.openclaw/tmp/` inside the workspace for scratch/temp/meta files that file tools need to read or write later.
memory flush append result: Appended content to memory/2026-06-06.md.
final target content after append:
  edited memory
  flush note
```

- Observed result after fix: Alias path file-tool write/read/edit succeeded through `memory/2026-06-06.md`; the alias symlink realpath exactly matched the configured target; direct writes to the outside target path still rejected; memory-triggered append-only write appended through the alias path.
- What was not tested: Full `pnpm check`/broad CI and a live deployed Gateway process. The added proof is a live local file-tool/memory-flush tool run from the PR branch, not a mocked assertion or screenshot.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Temporary filesystem paths are redacted in the terminal capture. This validates the runtime tool-construction and filesystem boundary behavior locally, but maintainers still need to accept the security semantics and host-only config scope.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing coverage already showed outside-workspace symlink parents reject under `workspaceOnly`; #90977 includes the observed deployed failure message: `openclaw.write` rejected `memory/2026-06-06.md` with `path alias escape blocked`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added config schema coverage for global and agent-level `tools.fs.workspaceAliases`.
- Added tool policy coverage for global alias inheritance and agent alias override.
- Added filesystem behavior coverage for configured outside-workspace symlink aliases, direct target rejection, and memory-triggered append-only writes through the alias.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The deployed repro in #90977 failed memory flush writes because `memory/2026-06-06.md` resolved outside the agent workspace through a symlink.
- Existing tests already asserted that outside-workspace symlink parents are rejected under `workspaceOnly`; this PR keeps that default and adds an explicit configured alias path.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Operators can opt into configured workspace aliases for filesystem tools when `workspaceOnly` is enabled.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This adds `tools.fs.workspaceAliases` to the config schema/types/help/labels/tags and docs. There is no migration because the default remains unchanged.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This changes filesystem tool execution behavior behind an explicit config allowlist.

What is the highest-risk area?

- The new config widens the effective workspace boundary for file tools if an operator configures an alias incorrectly.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Aliases are disabled by default.
- Alias `path` must be relative to the workspace.
- Alias `target` must be an absolute existing directory.
- The workspace alias path must realpath exactly to the configured target root.
- File tools must address files through the alias path; direct target paths still reject.
- Existing default outside-workspace symlink rejection remains covered.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer/product/security review of the config shape and boundary semantics.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Waiting on CI and maintainer product/security review.
- Added ClawSweeper-requested local live file-tool proof for alias success, direct-target rejection, and memory-triggered append-only write behavior. Full `pnpm check` or deployed Gateway proof can still be run if maintainers want it before merge.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- This PR follows ClawSweeper's issue guidance from #90977 by implementing a narrow exact-realpath alias policy instead of disabling `workspaceOnly` or broadening the whole workspace.

target.title: [AI-assisted] fix(cli): surface session lane busy exits
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: A same-session `openclaw agent` CLI dispatch can wait behind an active session lane, exit with code 1, and print no actionable error even though the Gateway already logs the lane-wait facts.
- Solution: Propagate lane-wait facts from the command queue into CLI-tracked agent runs, return a structured Gateway final payload, and render a clear CLI busy message.
- What changed: Added typed queue wait info, an additive `rejectOnWait` queue hook, a session-lane-busy error helper, CLI-only embedded-runner opt-in, Gateway final payload handling, CLI stderr/JSON rendering, and focused regression coverage.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No queue ordering change, no new CLI flag, no provider/channel/config/dependency change, and no change to non-CLI agent runs.

## Motivation

- Operators need to distinguish a blocked same-session lane from connection, model, or provider failures. The diagnostic data already exists; this patch makes the CLI caller receive it instead of a silent exit.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #54622
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A second CLI agent dispatch to the same session lane now receives a deterministic busy error when the first CLI dispatch keeps the lane occupied past the CLI timeout threshold.
- Real environment tested: Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout on fresh `openclaw/openclaw` main `a547010a957546d3e1c573cf2fed6aac9295f315`, Node v24.15.0, repo-managed pnpm, local mock OpenAI endpoint, real Gateway service process, and two real source-entrypoint CLI `agent` processes.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm check:test-types

LANE_BUSY_HOLD_MS=35000 LANE_BUSY_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=80 LANE_BUSY_FIRST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=60 LANE_BUSY_SECOND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=1 node lane-busy-cli-service-proof.mjs patched-long-active-provider80
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text

second CLI: exit=1, stdout empty
second CLI stderr:
Error: session lane busy (lane=session:agent:hana:main, waitedMs=999ms, queueAhead=0, activeAhead=1, activeNow=1, queueBehind=0)
Suggestion: wait for the current task to complete and retry.
providerRequestCount=1
```

- Observed result after fix: The second CLI dispatch fails before sending a second provider request, surfaces the lane as busy, and gives the operator a retry action.
- What was not tested: No external provider, channel, or built `dist` package binary was used. `openclaw.mjs --version` worked, but the Gateway/agent binary path needed `dist/entry.js`; `pnpm build` was killed by SIGKILL inside `tsdown` on the disposable test host. The real service proof therefore uses the source entrypoint (`node --import tsx src/entry.ts`). Full `pnpm check:changed` is not claimed because the local Crabbox binary failed its own sanity check, and a direct remote-child changed check exceeded the local 20 minute timeout during broad lint/type work.
- Before evidence:

```text
base-v2 commit=a547010a957546d3e1c573cf2fed6aac9295f315
first CLI: exit=0, stdout contains FIRST_DONE
second CLI: exit=0, stdout=SECOND_DONE
second CLI stderr empty
providerRequestCount=2

base-long-active-provider80
first CLI: exit=0, stdout contains FIRST_DONE
second CLI: no LANE_WAIT_EXCEEDED payload before Gateway final wait; Gateway timed out after 31000ms, then embedded fallback produced SECOND_DONE
providerRequestCount=3

queue before/after harness:
unpatched main: queuedTaskDidRun=true, queued outcome resolved queued-task-ran
patched branch: queuedTaskDidRun=false, queued outcome rejected AgentSessionLaneBusyError / LANE_WAIT_EXCEEDED
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The command queue already detects and logs lane waits, but that `onWait` path is diagnostic-only. CLI-tracked Gateway agent dispatches did not convert the wait event into a final response payload that the CLI relay could render.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no test that asserted a waited same-session CLI dispatch receives an actionable busy result instead of silently exiting.
- Contributing context (if known): The session lane serializer correctly protects same-session ordering; the missing piece was surfacing the existing wait state to the caller at the CLI timeout boundary.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/process/command-queue.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, and `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: A queued same-lane entry is rejected at the wait threshold while the active lane remains blocked, without running the queued task; Gateway returns a structured `LANE_WAIT_EXCEEDED` final payload for CLI task tracking; the CLI relay renders a clear stderr message and exits non-zero.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The queue, Gateway payload, and CLI relay are the three handoff points that previously dropped the lane-wait signal.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing lane serialization tests cover ordering, but not caller-visible lane-busy propagation.
- If no new test is added, why not: New tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

A blocked same-session CLI agent dispatch now prints an actionable error instead of silently exiting:

```text
Error: session lane busy (...)
Suggestion: wait for the current task to complete and retry.
```

JSON-mode callers receive a structured lane-busy response with `errorCode: "LANE_WAIT_EXCEEDED"` and lane wait details.

## Diagram

```text
CLI #1 -> Gateway -> session lane active -> provider request in flight
CLI #2 -> Gateway -> same session lane waits -> CLI timeout threshold
      -> Gateway final payload LANE_WAIT_EXCEEDED
      -> CLI stderr busy message, exit 1, no second provider request
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No; the patch prevents an extra provider call in the busy-lane case.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable test environment
- Runtime/container: Node v24.15.0, repo-managed pnpm
- Model/provider: Local mock OpenAI-compatible endpoint
- Integration/channel (if any): CLI agent relay through Gateway
- Relevant config (redacted): Token-auth local Gateway, one test agent, isolated temporary state, dummy provider key only

### Steps

1. Start a local Gateway service from the patched source entrypoint.
2. Dispatch CLI agent request #1 to `agent:hana:main` with a mock provider response delayed long enough to hold the lane.
3. Dispatch CLI agent request #2 to the same `agent:hana:main` session with a shorter timeout.
4. Compare base main and patched branch provider request counts and CLI output.

### Expected

- The second CLI dispatch reports the same-session lane as busy and exits non-zero without reaching the provider.

### Actual

- Patched branch: second CLI dispatch exits 1 with a session lane busy message and `providerRequestCount=1`.
- Unpatched main: second CLI dispatch waits, reaches the provider, returns `SECOND_DONE`, and `providerRequestCount=2`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
Focused tests:
Gateway agent tests: 302/302 PASS
Command queue tests: 32/32 PASS
CLI relay tests: 56/56 PASS
Adjacent embedded-agent-runner lanes tests: 6/6 PASS

Static checks:
git diff --check PASS
oxfmt changed files PASS
targeted type-aware oxlint PASS, 0 warnings, 0 errors
pnpm check:test-types PASS

Codex review:
Attempted locally before PR creation, but the Windows host denied executing codex.exe. No Codex-review green claim is made.
```

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Base-vs-patched same-session CLI/Gateway service behavior, queue-level before/after behavior, Gateway final payload shape, CLI stderr rendering, CLI JSON lane-busy handling, formatting, lint, and test-types.
- Edge cases checked: The queued task is not executed after lane-busy rejection; the next queued entry can still drain after a rejected wait entry; the CLI opt-in uses the agent timeout threshold rather than the queue's ordinary warning threshold.
- What you did **not** verify: Full `pnpm check:changed`, built `dist` package binary, external providers, external channels, or a live user account.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No review conversations exist yet.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Not applicable.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Normal short same-session waits could be rejected too aggressively.
  - Mitigation: The CLI opt-in uses the agent run timeout threshold, not the command queue's ordinary wait-warning threshold.
- Risk: Non-CLI agent surfaces could receive a new busy behavior unexpectedly.
  - Mitigation: `failOnSessionLaneWait` is an internal opt-in enabled for CLI task tracking only.
- Risk: Busy details could expose too much context.
  - Mitigation: The error includes lane identity and queue timing/counts only; it does not include prompts, provider payloads, secrets, or message content.

target.title: fix(daemon): write LaunchAgent plist to boot volume when home is on external APFS
target.body:
## Summary

macOS `launchd` refuses to bootstrap a LaunchAgent plist that lives on an external APFS volume (`Bootstrap failed: 5: Input/output error`). When a user's `$HOME` is on an external drive, `openclaw gateway install` always fails.

This PR makes the macOS LaunchAgent path resolution **boot-volume-aware**. A single helper, `resolveLaunchAgentHomeDir(env)` (in `src/daemon/paths.ts`), returns `$HOME` normally but falls back to the boot-volume home `/Users/<user>` when `$HOME` is on an external volume (detected by comparing `statSync("/").dev` vs `statSync(home).dev`). Every launchd lifecycle path derives from this one source, so install, repair, restart, uninstall, status/audit, and doctor service detection all converge on the same path launchd can actually bootstrap.

### Why this shape (vs. an install-only copy)

An earlier revision wrote a second boot-volume plist only at install time. That left `repair`, `restart`, and `uninstall` still bootstrapping from the external path (which fails with the same error 5), and split state across two plists. Making the resolver canonical avoids a second path and keeps every lifecycle command consistent. Correctness details handled:

- **Uninstall trash** now targets `/Users/<user>/.Trash` (same volume as the plist) instead of the external `$HOME/.Trash`; a cross-volume `rename` would `EXDEV`-fail and leak the plist.
- **Doctor service detection** (`src/daemon/inspect.ts`) scans the boot-volume `Library/LaunchAgents` so an external-home install is actually detected.
- **Detached restart handoff** (`src/daemon/launchd-restart-handoff.ts`) routes through the same resolver, so an in-band reload/kickstart fallback bootstraps the boot-volume plist instead of re-hitting error 5 against the external volume.
- **Supervisor restart fallback** (`src/infra/restart.ts`) and the **update restart helper script** (`src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts`) also `launchctl bootstrap` the plist; both now resolve through the same boot-volume-aware home. Logs and state stay under the user's real `$HOME`.
- The boot-volume account folder is derived from the **login identity** (`USER`/`LOGNAME`, else the process owner via `os.userInfo()`), not from `$HOME`'s structure — the reported `$HOME` is the external volume root itself (`/Volumes/MainDataDrive`), whose last path segment is the volume name, not the user. Case is preserved.
- The external-volume decision is memoized (single slot; a path's volume is process-stable), so the status/restart polling loops do not re-`stat` on every poll.

**Behavior on a normal Mac (HOME on the boot volume): unchanged.** `/` and `$HOME` report the same `st_dev` (macOS firmlinks), so the resolver returns `$HOME` exactly as before — no extra plist, no path change.

**Changed files:**
- `src/daemon/paths.ts` — `resolveLaunchAgentHomeDir()` + memoized external-volume detection.
- `src/daemon/launchd.ts` — plist path, plist write, and uninstall trash all derive from the boot-volume-aware home (removed the install-only double-write helpers).
- `src/daemon/inspect.ts` — darwin user-scope service scan uses the boot-volume-aware home.
- `src/daemon/launchd-restart-handoff.ts` — detached restart plist path uses the boot-volume-aware home.
- `src/infra/restart.ts` / `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts` — supervisor and update restart bootstrap fallbacks use the boot-volume-aware home.
- Tests: external-volume canonical-path test using a real external `/Volumes/.../Users/test` HOME, plus uninstall-trash, restart-handoff, supervisor-restart, and update-restart-script external-HOME regression tests.

Rebased onto current `main`.

## Verification

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/launchd.test.ts   # 82/82 passed
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/inspect.test.ts    # passed
oxfmt --check / oxlint / tsgo (prod types)                # clean
```

### Real behavior proof (live macOS launchd + real APFS volume)

- **Behavior addressed:** `openclaw gateway install` fails with `Bootstrap failed: 5: Input/output error` when `$HOME` is on an external APFS volume, because launchd cannot bootstrap a plist from that volume. The fix resolves the plist (and its lifecycle siblings) to the boot volume.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS (Darwin 24.6.0), real APFS disk image attached as an external volume (`hdiutil`), real `launchctl` in the `gui/<uid>` domain, and the real `resolveLaunchAgentPlistPath` from `src/daemon/launchd.ts`. A harmless plist running `/usr/bin/true` was used so no real gateway was started; everything was booted out and the image detached afterward.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Create + attach a real external APFS volume; confirm its `st_dev` differs from `/`.
  2. `launchctl bootstrap gui/$UID <plist-on-external-volume>` → observe error 5.
  3. `launchctl bootstrap gui/$UID <plist-on-boot-volume>` → observe success + `launchctl print`.
  4. Run the patched `resolveLaunchAgentPlistPath` with `HOME` on the external volume and assert it returns the boot-volume path.
- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
=== 1. Real external APFS volume ===
root '/' device id: 16777220
'/Volumes/OCExtHome' device id: 16777234
-> confirmed: external volume is a DIFFERENT device than root

=== 3. BEFORE FIX behavior: bootstrap FROM the external volume ===
$ launchctl bootstrap gui/501 /Volumes/OCExtHome/Users/<user>/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.proof.exthome60398.plist
Bootstrap failed: 5: Input/output error
launchctl exit code: 5   (the reported #60398 failure)

=== 4. AFTER FIX behavior: bootstrap FROM the boot volume ===
$ launchctl bootstrap gui/501 /Users/<user>/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.proof.exthome60398.plist
launchctl exit code: 0   (success)
$ launchctl print gui/501/ai.openclaw.proof.exthome60398
	path = /Users/<user>/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.proof.exthome60398.plist
	state = not running
	program = /usr/bin/true

=== patched resolver against the real external volume (both HOME shapes) ===
external volume device: 16777234, root device: 16777220
nested external HOME: HOME=/Volumes/OCExtHome2/Users/<user>
  -> /Users/<user>/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist PASS
volume-root external HOME (#60398 shape): HOME=/Volumes/OCExtHome2
  -> /Users/<user>/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist PASS
ALL PASS: both external HOME shapes resolve to the boot-volume plist (derived
from the login user, not the volume name)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** bootstrapping the plist from the external APFS volume reproduces `Bootstrap failed: 5: Input/output error`; the identical plist on the boot volume bootstraps with exit code 0 and is visible via `launchctl print`; and the patched `resolveLaunchAgentPlistPath` redirects an external `$HOME` to the boot-volume path while leaving a normal boot-volume `$HOME` unchanged.
- **What was not tested:** a full end-to-end `openclaw gateway install` of the real gateway service on an external home (avoided to not register a real launchd gateway on the test machine); a physically separate USB/Thunderbolt enclosure (an attached APFS disk image was used, which is a real, distinct APFS volume with a distinct `st_dev`).

Fixes #60398

## Summary

- Problem: Agents have workspace directories but no way to browse or manage files through the Control UI.
- Why it matters: Users need to inspect, edit, and organize agent workspace files without SSH or CLI access.
- What changed: Added `agents.workspace.*` gateway RPC methods (list, get, set, delete, mkdir, move, stat) and a Workspace tab in the agents panel with file browser, editor, and file operations.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to agent runtime behavior, plugin SDK, or existing file/config panels.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix

- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations

- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #61368
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/agents-workspace.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Path traversal rejection, CRUD operations, file size limits, error handling
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Unit tests cover all API methods with mocked fs, validating security and correctness without requiring a running gateway
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None (new feature)
- If no new test is added, why not: Tests are included

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New "Workspace" tab in the agents panel for browsing and managing agent workspace files
- File browser with directory navigation, breadcrumbs, and sorting (directories first)
- Inline file editor with dirty state tracking and save/delete actions
- File upload, download, and mkdir operations
- 10MB file size limit enforced server-side

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[user] -> [agents panel] -> [overview | files | tools | skills | channels | cron]

After:
[user] -> [agents panel] -> [overview | files | workspace | tools | skills | channels | cron]
                                          |
                                    [file tree] + [file editor]
                                          |
                              agents.workspace.* RPC -> fs-safe
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes - workspace file read/write through gateway RPC
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes - new `agents.workspace.*` RPC methods
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? Yes - workspace directory access scoped per agent
- Risk + mitigation: All file operations use `fs-safe` helpers with path traversal protection. Relative paths are validated to reject `..` and absolute paths. File size capped at 10MB. Operations are scoped to the resolved agent workspace directory.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Runtime/container: Docker (openclaw-workspace-test:latest)
- Model/provider: N/A (UI feature)
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: Default gateway config with `--allow-unconfigured --bind lan`

### Steps

1. Start gateway with `openclaw gateway --allow-unconfigured`
2. Open Control UI and navigate to an agent
3. Click the "Workspace" tab
4. Browse files, select a file to edit, modify content, click Save

### Expected

- File tree shows workspace contents with correct icons and sizes
- Editing a file enables the Save button (dirty state)
- Clicking Save persists changes and disables the button
- Navigating away resets editor state

### Actual

- All operations work as expected

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Unit tests: 40+ test cases covering security (path traversal), CRUD, edge cases, and error handling all pass.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: File browse, edit, save, delete, mkdir, navigate, upload via Docker container with mounted ~/.openclaw
- Edge cases checked: Path traversal attempts, empty directories, large file rejection, file selection reset on navigate
- What I did not verify: Base64 encoding upload through UI, symlink display

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: New RPC surface increases attack surface for path traversal

target.title: fix(stream): recover visible content when reasoning_delta straddles an inline code span (closes #96869)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a reasoning model emits a single streaming chunk carrying both `content` (visible) and `reasoning_content` deltas, and the model crosses modes mid-inline-code-span (the visible delta ends with an opener single backtick with no closer, and the reasoning delta carries the matching closer), the visible content is persisted with an **unclosed inline code span** and the matching half + everything after is silently routed into a private `thinking` block. Users see a reply truncated mid-sentence at a backtick; the remainder is hidden and never displayed.

Ref: #96869 (issue with full root-cause analysis and a runnable TS repro against upstream source).

## Root cause

In `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts` and `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, when a chunk carries both deltas, `appendPartitionedContent` routes the `content` delta through `reasoningTagTextPartitioner.push|pushVisible`, but `appendThinkingDelta` writes the `reasoning_content` delta **directly to the thinking block, bypassing the partitioner**. The partitioner never sees the closer backtick, so the visible block is finalized with an unclosed inline code span and the matching half is locked into a private thinking block.

The partitioner itself correctly tracks inline-code state across chunks (its existing tests cover cross-chunk code spans — see `preserves code-span reasoning tag examples when the closing backtick arrives later`). The bug is that `appendThinkingDelta` doesn't consult that state.

## Why This Change Was Made

Without this fix, any OpenAI-compatible reasoning provider that interleaves `content` and `reasoning_content` (Venice `claude-opus-4-8`, `zai-org-glm-5-2`, OpenAI o-series, qwen3-style servers, Anthropic-via-OpenAI proxies, etc.) can silently lose user-visible reply content. The failure is intermittent but message-loss-class.

## User Impact

- **Before:** reasoning-model replies could be persisted truncated mid-sentence at an unclosed backtick. The remainder lived in a `thinking` block in the session log and was never rendered to the user. No error was surfaced to operators or users.
- **After:** the partitioner is consulted before writing a reasoning delta to the thinking block. If the visible stream is mid-unclosed inline code span, the reasoning delta is routed as visible text and the open code span closes naturally. Existing reasoning flows (reasoningDepth > 0, or visible stream balanced) are byte-identical.

Provider-agnostic — fixes any reasoning provider routed through `openai-completions` or `openai-transport-stream`.

## Evidence

### Repro (before → after)

Running the exact failure scenario against the partitioner, simulating the routing order in `openai-completions.ts`:

```ts
const partitioner = createReasoningTagTextPartitioner();
const visible: string[] = [];
const thinking: string[] = [];

// One streaming chunk where the provider emits BOTH:
//   choice.delta.content         = "Visible. It uses a `"      // ends with opener backtick, no closer
//   choice.delta.reasoning_content = " blocks) and more"        // model crossed back to thinking mid-span
partitioner.markStrict();
for (const d of partitioner.push("Visible. It uses a `")) {
  (d.kind === "text" ? visible : thinking).push(d.text);
}
thinking.push(" blocks) and more"); // simulate appendThinkingDelta bypassing partitioner
for (const d of partitioner.flush()) {
  (d.kind === "text" ? visible : thinking).push(d.text);
}
```

- **Before this PR:** visible = `"Visible. It uses a \``" (1 backtick, unclosed), thinking = `" blocks) and more"` — content lost from visible.
- **After this PR:** visible = `"Visible. It uses a \` blocks) and more"` (2 backticks, balanced), no thinking block.

### Real-world failure (before)

Observed on OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (npm global), Venice `claude-opus-4-8` and `zai-org-glm-5-2`. Persisted assistant reply (501 chars, truncated at unclosed backtick):

> Got the full page now. Here's the honest read — and your "better than GLM" claim is *half* right in a way that actually makes it more interesting, not less.
>
> ## What Ornith-1.0-397B actually is
>
> - **397B MoE**, post-trained on Gemma 4 + Qwen 3.5
> - **MIT licensed** — fully open, no regional restrictions
> - **Built for agentic coding** — tool calling, terminal agents, SWE-bench. It's a reasoning model (`

The matching half was stored in a `thinking` block in the OpenClaw session, never displayed:

> ` blocks).
> - Notably: it explicitly lists **OpenClaw** and **OpenCode** as supported harnesses. This model is *built* for the exact kind of work we do.

### Tests added

- `src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.test.ts` — 2 tests for the new `isInsideUnclosedVisibleInlineCode()` (unclosed across chunks, false while inside reasoning).
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` — regression test for the exact chunk shape in the repro: content ending in opener backtick + reasoning-only chunk with the continuation. Asserts visible text `"Visible. It uses a \` blocks) and more"` with balanced backticks and **no** `thinking` block.

### Test results (local lane, on `origin/main` @ 95b97e5b0b)

- `pnpm test src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.test.ts` → 28 / 28 pass (26 existing + 2 new)
- `pnpm test src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` → 32 / 32 pass (31 existing + 1 new regression)
- `pnpm test src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` → 266 pass / 10 fail — **the 10 failures are pre-existing on clean `main`** (verified by stashing this change: same 266 pass / 10 fail). All unrelated DeepSeek DSML / ModelStudio / Qwen / DashScope streaming-usage tests.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs` → clean exit, no type errors.
- `pnpm build` (tsdown) → success; dist bundles contain the new `isInsideUnclosedVisibleInlineCode` symbol.

## AI-assisted

Yes — this PR was authored with AI assistance. The diagnosis, repro, fix, and tests were produced with an AI coding agent (Eva / OpenClaw). I (the human author) reviewed the diff and the test results.

## Checklist

- [x] Marked AI-assisted above
- [x] Evidence included (repro + real-world failure + test results)
- [x] Focused PR (one bug, one fix lane: 3 source files + 2 test files)
- [x] Existing partitioner + provider tests still pass
- [x] No CHANGELOG edits (per CONTRIBUTING)
- [x] No refactor-only or unrelated changes
- [x] Branch is on a fork; "Allow edits by maintainers" left enabled (default for fork PRs)
- [x] American English spelling in code/comments

Happy to revise per maintainer review. The two valid fix directions discussed in the issue were (a) route the `reasoning_content` delta through the partitioner as well, or (b) guard `appendThinkingDelta`. This PR implements (b) — the more surgical fix that preserves the partitioner's current behavior for genuine reasoning flows. If (a) is preferred, I can revise.
